local_names.romanized_local: Kao Ji Chi Pai Pan (Mandarin romanization)

food_subgroup: Grilled meat platter with salad and starch

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no specific platter or mixed-dish entry exists. Closest parent used as the defensible anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.foodex2_description: Poultry meat, cooked — grilled chicken wing component of mixed platter
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked poultry meat. The full platter includes salad and starch components not captured by a single FoodEx2 code; the dominant protein component is used as the anchor with closest_parent match type.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a composite grilled chicken wing platter dish.

serving.description: 1 platter with chicken wings, salad, and bread or rice
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard platter serving of approximately 600 g, comprising grilled chicken mid wings, a fresh mixed salad, and a choice of hot bread or steamed rice.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, wing, meat and skin, cooked, roasted (SR Legacy ID 05072) and mixed salad/rice components
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled/roasted chicken wing (meat and skin), mixed salad vegetables, and cooked rice/bread components, weighted to reflect the platter composition. Input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against USDA data and retained where consistent. Singapore HPB Foodo database was also consulted for regional dish context. Energy 158 kcal/100g, protein 10.8 g, fat 9.2 g, carbohydrate 8.3 g are plausible for a mixed platter of this composition and are consistent with USDA component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for mixed grilled chicken and rice/bread platter
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a grilled chicken wing platter. GI estimated at 56 (medium) based on the weighted contribution of the starch components: white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database) and white bread/pita (GI ~75), offset by the protein and fat content of grilled chicken wings and the low-GI salad vegetables. Assuming approximately 40% of the 600 g serving is starch (bread or rice), the blended dish GI is estimated at ~56. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 56 × available carbohydrate per serving (49.98 g from 600 g × 8.33 g/100g) / 100 = 28.0 g (rounded to 27.9).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 200 mg/100 g; a full 600 g platter delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, representing 52% of the WHO daily limit of 2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation, limit pita bread to one piece, verify that spice blends do not contain added salt, and balance the platter's sodium load against other meals in the day; the potassium contribution from salad vegetables (~200 mg/100 g estimated) provides partial DASH-diet offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Mixed GI platter (estimated GI 56, medium); glycemic load per 600 g serving is approximately 28 g, which is significant for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should choose the rice or bread option in a controlled portion (half serving of starch), prioritise the grilled chicken and salad components, avoid sweet dipping sauces, and monitor post-meal blood glucose given the moderate glycemic load of the full platter.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.5 g/100 g and cholesterol 41.67 mg/100 g; a full 600 g serving delivers approximately 15 g saturated fat and 250 mg cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should request skin-removed chicken wings to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol, choose olive-oil-based dressing over cream sauces, limit the bread component to reduce refined carbohydrate load, and note that the grilled preparation method is preferable to fried alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory olive oil and herbs; moderate saturated fat; nightshade ingredients (tomato, paprika) may be a concern for solanine-sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory properties of olive oil and polyphenol-rich herbs; however, those with solanine sensitivity should request tomato and pepper to be omitted from the salad, and should request skin-removed chicken to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Grilled Chicken Wing Platter is a Singaporean restaurant dish featuring grilled chicken mid wings served alongside a fresh mixed salad (typically comprising lettuce, cucumber, tomato, and onion) and a customer's choice of hot bread (pita or similar) or steamed rice. The dish is prepared by marinating chicken wings in a blend of olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and dried herbs such as oregano and paprika, then grilling over direct heat until the skin is lightly charred and the meat is cooked through. The result is a juicy, mildly smoky protein component with a crisp exterior, paired with the cool crunch of fresh salad and the satisfying starch of bread or rice. The platter is listed as halal and is influenced by Turkish kebab and shawarma culinary traditions, reflecting Singapore's multicultural food landscape. Nutritionally, the platter provides approximately 158 kcal per 100 g, with 10.8 g protein, 9.2 g fat (2.5 g saturated), and 8.3 g carbohydrate. A standard 600 g serving delivers roughly 950 kcal, 65 g protein, 55 g fat, and 50 g carbohydrate. Sodium is 200 mg/100 g (1200 mg per full platter), placing it in the moderate concern range for hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium) due to the starch components, with a glycemic load of approximately 28 g per serving. Clinically, the dish is suitable for most adults in moderate portions; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should control starch portions, those with hypertension should request reduced salt, and those with cardiovascular disease should request skin-removed wings to lower saturated fat intake.

confidence_reason: Input record was ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from a delivery platform (grabfood), which is a forbidden primary nutrient source. Nutrient values were cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for grilled chicken wing (meat and skin) and platter components and found to be plausible and consistent. Values are retained as adapted estimates. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (grilled/roasted chicken wing, meat and skin, ID 05072) and component estimates for mixed salad and rice/bread, adapted to match the platter composition. The original input values (grabfood, ai_generated) were cross-checked and found consistent with USDA data; they are retained as adapted estimates. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.2) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fatty acid profile for roasted chicken wing and are approximate for the mixed platter. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite platter dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024; component-level data exists but reliable weighted aggregation for this specific platter formulation is not possible without recipe-level detail. 4. potassium_mg (200.0) is an estimate based on USDA data for salad vegetables and chicken wing components; this value is approximate. 5. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific platter exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The input cultural tags (Turkish, Kebab, Shawarma) reflect the dish's culinary influence; the country of origin is Singapore. 7. The dish is listed as halal per the input dietary_tags. 8. Allergen note: the bread option contains gluten/wheat; the rice option is gluten-free. 9. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2, as no exact single-code match exists for a composite mixed platter dish.
